Material.title: Material
Material.description: Represents a structural material with its physical properties, which can be preloaded or custom-defined by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Material entity.

name.description: Common name or designation of the material (e.g., 'A36 Steel', 'C30 Concrete', 'Douglas Fir Timber').

youngsModulus.description: Modulus of Elasticity (E) of the material. Units are context-dependent and handled by the app's unit conversion.

yieldStrength.description: Yield strength of the material, primarily relevant for ductile materials like steel. Units are context-dependent.

ultimateStrength.description: Ultimate tensile strength of the material. Units are context-dependent.

compressiveStrength.description: Compressive strength of the material, primarily relevant for brittle materials like concrete. Units are context-dependent.

density.description: Mass density of the material. Units are context-dependent.

poissonsRatio.description: Poisson's ratio (v) of the material, indicating lateral strain properties (unitless).

isCustom.description: Indicates if this material was added by a user (true) or is a preloaded default material (false).

createdAt.description: Date and time when the material entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time when the material entry was last updated.

Section.title: Section
Section.description: Represents a specific structural cross-section with its geometric dimensions and calculated properties. Can be preloaded or custom-defined.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Section entity.

name.description: User-friendly name or designation for the specific section (e.g., 'W14x22 I-Beam', 'Circular Column 300mm', 'Custom Rectangle').

shapeType.description: The geometric shape category of the section (e.g., 'Rectangle', 'I-Beam', 'Circle', 'Angle', 'Channel').

description.description: An optional detailed description or notes about the section.

width.description: Overall width of the section. Applicable for various shapes (e.g., breadth of a rectangle, flange width of an I-beam).

height.description: Overall height or depth of the section. Applicable for various shapes (e.g., depth of a rectangle, overall depth of an I-beam).

thickness.description: Thickness for plate-like or thin-walled sections, or a general dimension for simple shapes like a solid circle.

flangeWidth.description: Width of the flange for sections like I-beams, T-beams, or channels.

flangeThickness.description: Thickness of the flange for sections like I-beams, T-beams, or channels.

webThickness.description: Thickness of the web for sections like I-beams, T-beams, or channels.

area.description: Calculated cross-sectional area of the section. Units are context-dependent.

momentOfInertiaX.description: Calculated moment of inertia about the X-axis (major axis). Units are context-dependent.

momentOfInertiaY.description: Calculated moment of inertia about the Y-axis (minor axis). Units are context-dependent.

sectionModulusX.description: Calculated section modulus about the X-axis. Units are context-dependent.

sectionModulusY.description: Calculated section modulus about the Y-axis. Units are context-dependent.

isCustom.description: Indicates if this section was added by a user (true) or is a preloaded standard section (false).

createdAt.description: Date and time when the section entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time when the section entry was last updated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Organizes collections of related structural calculations for a specific engineering project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

name.description: User-defined name for the project (e.g., 'Office Building Annex', 'Bridge Repair Phase 1').

description.description: A brief description or notes about the project.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the project was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time when the project was last updated.

Calculation.title: Calculation
Calculation.description: Represents a single instance of a saved structural calculation, including its inputs, results, and context.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Calculation entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this calculation belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Calculation)

name.description: User-defined name for this specific calculation instance (e.g., 'Beam Deflection - Floor 3, Grid Line A').

calculationType.description: The category or type of structural calculation performed (e.g., 'BeamDesign', 'ColumnDesign', 'LoadCombination', 'StressStrain', 'CustomFormula').

description.description: A detailed description of the calculation scenario, assumptions, or specific conditions.

inputs.description: A serialized JSON object or string containing all input parameters for the calculation. Its structure varies based on 'calculationType'.

results.description: A serialized JSON object or string containing all computed output results from the calculation. Its structure varies based on 'calculationType'.

materialId.description: Optional reference to the Material used in this calculation. (Relationship: Material 1:N Calculation)

sectionId.description: Optional reference to the Section used in this calculation. (Relationship: Section 1:N Calculation)

customFormulaId.description: Optional reference to the CustomFormula used for this calculation. (Relationship: CustomFormula 1:N Calculation)

createdAt.description: Date and time when the calculation was saved.

updatedAt.description: Date and time when the calculation was last updated.

CustomFormula.title: CustomFormula
CustomFormula.description: Allows users to define and save their own mathematical formulas for future use in calculations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomFormula entity.

name.description: User-defined name for the custom formula (e.g., 'My Special Beam Formula', 'Custom Stress Check').

description.description: A brief description of what the custom formula calculates or its purpose.

formulaExpression.description: The mathematical expression of the formula, often in a parseable string format (e.g., 'P * L^3 / (48 * E * I)').

inputParameters.description: A list of variable names (e.g., ['P', 'L', 'E', 'I']) expected as inputs for the formula.

outputParameter.description: The name of the resulting output variable calculated by the formula (e.g., 'Deflection', 'Moment').

createdAt.description: Date and time when the custom formula was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time when the custom formula was last updated.

definition.description: Stores preloaded, publicly available structural materials. These are read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

params.name: materialId
params.description: The unique identifier for a public material.

definition.description: Stores preloaded, publicly available structural cross-sections. These are read-only for all authenticated users and managed by administrators.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a public section.

definition.description: Designates users with administrative privileges. The existence of a document here grants admin access for managing public data. This follows the DBAC pattern of 'existence over content'.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of an administrative user.

definition.description: Stores custom structural materials created by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). The `isCustom` field should be true. Includes implicit denormalization of ownerId via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the material's owner.

params.name: materialId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user-created custom material.

definition.description: Stores custom structural cross-sections created by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). The `isCustom` field should be true. Includes implicit denormalization of ownerId via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the section's owner.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user-created custom section.

definition.description: Stores custom formulas defined and saved by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). Includes implicit denormalization of ownerId via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the custom formula's owner.

params.name: customFormulaId
params.description: The unique identifier for a user-defined custom formula.

definition.description: Stores user-owned projects, acting as containers for related calculations. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). Includes implicit denormalization of ownerId via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the project's owner.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for a project.

definition.description: Stores individual calculation instances, nested under their parent project. Access is restricted to the project owner (`userId`). The `projectId` field within the document must match the path's `projectId` for data integrity. Includes implicit denormalization of ownerId via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the project owner (and thus the calculation owner).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent project.

params.name: calculationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific calculation within the project.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide secure, scalable, and debuggable access to all application data, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure achieves Authorization Independence by completely avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies that would require `get()` calls in security rules. All user-specific data (Projects, Custom Materials, Custom Sections, Custom Formulas, and their nested Calculations) are organized under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This ensures that the ownership (`request.auth.uid == userId`) can be verified directly from the document's path, eliminating the need to fetch parent documents. For instance, a `Calculation`'s access is determined by its path `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/calculations/{calculationId}`, where `userId` from the path must match `request.auth.uid`, making its authorization self-contained within its full path.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:** To satisfy Strategy B, preloaded (public) materials and sections are segregated into dedicated top-level collections: `/materials_public` and `/sections_public`. These collections have a uniform security posture (read-only for all authenticated users, writable only by administrators). User-defined (custom) materials and sections are stored under the user's specific path, e.g., `/users/{userId}/custom_materials`, ensuring their security posture is private and owner-specific. This segregation simplifies security rules significantly as each collection's ruleset is clear and consistent.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This design directly supports secure `list` operations. Because collections are segregated by security posture and ownership, a user querying `/users/{userId}/projects` will only ever see *their own* projects, as the rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId`. Similarly, a `list` on `/materials_public` will return all public materials to any authenticated user. There is no need for Firestore rules to filter results; the structure inherently limits the scope of queries to authorized data.

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** Administrative roles for managing public data are implemented using a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. The existence of a document at this path for a given `uid` grants administrative privileges, allowing rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`. This keeps roles within the database, as mandated.

**Denormalization:** While `projectId` is present in the `Calculation` document, this is primarily for data integrity and efficient querying, not for authorization rules, as the `projectId` is already part of the path. The `userId` is implicitly denormalized through the path structure for all user-owned data. This ensures atomic creation and updates without complex `get()` rule evaluations.

Overall, this structure provides a clear, maintainable, and secure foundation for the StructoCalc application, prioritizing explicit authorization and efficient rule evaluation.
